description_long: This is a traditional stone water well with a circular stone base and a wooden canopy roof supported by four wooden posts. The roof is made of overlapping, weathered tiles arranged in a pitched style, providing shelter over the well. The well features a horizontal wooden beam with a central spindle, likely for winding a rope or chain to draw water, and two side-mounted crank handles. The stonework of the well is visibly textured, with individual stones clearly defined and mortared together. The structure sits on a square patch of grass, suggesting an outdoor placement. The overall design is utilitarian, with no visible decorative elements, focusing on durability and function.
description: A stone water well with a tiled wooden canopy roof, supported by four posts and equipped with a central spindle and crank handles. The well sits on a grassy base and is designed for practical water drawing.
description_view_0: Front view showing the wooden canopy, central spindle, and one crank handle, with the stone well partially visible behind the wooden beam.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the open front of the well, the circular stone structure, and the tiled roof extending over the well.
description_view_2: Rear view similar to the front, showing the wooden canopy, spindle, and crank handle, with the stone well partially obscured.
description_view_3: Opposite side view displaying the open access to the well, the stone structure, and the full span of the tiled roof.
attribute_presence_of_pulley_or_bucket_m: A central spindle with side crank handles is present, made of wood and metal, but no bucket or rope is visible.
attribute_surrounding_protective_structu: A wooden canopy roof supported by four posts covers the well, with a horizontal wooden beam acting as a partial barrier.
